findings.evidence: `rg -n "mapₗᵢ|hasSum_inner_mul_inner|summable_inner_mul_inner|tsum_inner_mul_inner" code/TauCeti` hits only `repr_mapₗᵢ`, `mapₗᵢ_apply`, `coe_mapₗᵢ`, `mapₗᵢ_refl`, and `mapₗᵢ_trans`; Mathlib only provides the generic `HilbertBasis.hasSum_inner_mul_inner`, `summable_inner_mul_inner`, and `tsum_inner_mul_inner` in `Mathlib/Analysis/InnerProductSpace/l2Space.lean:457-469`.
findings.fix: Add named lemmas such as `mapₗᵢ_hasSum_inner_mul_inner`, `mapₗᵢ_summable_inner_mul_inner`, and `mapₗᵢ_tsum_inner_mul_inner` rewriting the series over `e (b i)` or equivalently over `b i` after `e.symm`, with appropriate simp/automation annotations if they are intended normal forms.
findings.issue: `HilbertBasis.mapₗᵢ` exposes only repr/apply/refl/trans lemmas, but no transported `HasSum`, `Summable`, or `tsum` Parseval forms for `(b.mapₗᵢ e)`.

summary: The exposed `mapₗᵢ` API still lacks the advertised transported Parseval consumer lemmas, so users must unfold/rewrite the generic Mathlib statement themselves.
